income_thresholds.description: BPL and economically weaker families with income below Rs 3 lakh get free enrollment for Rs 2.5 lakh basic coverage and Rs 4 lakh critical illness coverage.
income_thresholds.source: Goa State Government / Directorate of Health Services Goa

income_thresholds.description: All Goa domicile holders are eligible for basic tier (Rs 2.5 lakh). No income cap for basic hospitalization coverage. Higher income families may need to pay nominal contribution.

exclusion_rules.description: Only residents of Goa with a valid Goa domicile certificate are eligible
Valid Goa domicile certificate is mandatory for enrollment. Non-domicile holders cannot enroll.
Families already covered under CGHS or ESIC are excluded from DDSSY
Pre-existing conditions may have a waiting period of 30 days from enrollment before coverage activates
Critical illness coverage capped at Rs 4 lakh per family per year. Claims beyond this limit are not covered.

family_criteria.family_definition: Family as defined in ration card. All members listed on ration card with Goa domicile are covered. Coverage is floater across all family members.

covered_procedures: Orthopaedic (knee replacement, hip replacement, fracture fixation)
Critical illness treatment (up to Rs 4 lakh)
Diagnostics and lab tests during hospitalization
excluded_procedures: Dental treatment (unless related to covered surgery)

required_documents.name: Aadhaar Card
required_documents.notes: Required for identity verification and e-KYC

required_documents.name: Goa Domicile Certificate
required_documents.notes: Mandatory. Issued by Mamlatdar office. Proves 15+ years of residence in Goa.

required_documents.name: Ration Card
required_documents.notes: Used to identify family members and economic status. BPL ration card holders get priority enrollment.

required_documents.name: Mobile Number
required_documents.notes: For OTP verification and scheme communication

enrollment_steps: Step 1: Visit the nearest government hospital, PHC, or DDSSY enrollment camp with required documents.
Step 2: Submit Aadhaar card, Goa domicile certificate, ration card, and photograph.
Step 3: Undergo biometric verification (fingerprint/iris scan) for e-KYC.
Step 4: DDSSY card is generated with family member details.
Step 5: Card is activated. Present at any empanelled hospital in Goa for cashless treatment.
Step 6: For basic hospitalization, coverage up to Rs 2.5 lakh. For critical illness, additional Rs 1.5 lakh (total Rs 4 lakh).
description_for_embedding: Deen Dayal Swasthya Seva Yojana (DDSSY) is Goa's universal state health insurance scheme providing Rs 2.5 lakh per family per year for basic hospitalization and Rs 4 lakh for critical illnesses. Uniquely, DDSSY has no income cap for the basic tier - all Goa domicile holders are eligible regardless of income. The scheme covers hospitalization, surgeries, critical illness treatment, maternity care, and day-care procedures at empanelled government and private hospitals across Goa. Goa domicile certificate is mandatory for enrollment. BPL families get priority and free enrollment. The scheme particularly benefits fishermen, tourism workers, mining workers, and other sections of Goa's population.
